items.title: Manmohan Singh Heterodox Economist
items.content: Ajit Singh and Pritam Singh had reached an understanding over several discussions that although the historical moment of the July 1991 balance of payments crisis in India made Manmohan Singh appear as a votary of market liberalisation, he was, in fact, a heterodox economist who understood the limitations of the market economy.

items.title: Letter: Singh's record does not fit easy left-right pigeonholing
items.content: Some rightwing and leftwing narratives on Manmohan Singh's economic vision are wrong. His record defies easy categorisation along conventional left-right lines.

items.title: Manmohan Singh Was More Than an Architect of India's Economic Reforms
items.content: The often-projected narrative does injustice to Singh's more expansive vision that guided many of his egalitarian policies. He was much more than a market reformer.

items.title: UK poll verdict more a Tory defeat than a Labour victory
items.content: The overwhelming conclusion that emerges from the UK general election results is that it is more of a defeat of the Conservative Party and less of a victory for the Labour Party. The protest vote against the Conservative government ensured a massive Labour majority.

items.title: BJP's electoral strategy: its inner contradictions could lead to its failure in 2024
items.content: As India's 2024 General Election gets underway, the emerging political scene throws signals of inner contradictions in the Modi-led BJP's strategy. What led this strategy to succeed in the past is precisely what may lead to its failure in 2024.

items.title: Surjit Patar, a poet for all seasons
items.content: A tribute to the eminent Punjabi poet Surjit Pattar, whose poetry touched universal human themes with a distinctly Punjabi sensibility.

items.title: Remembering Salvador Allende's Socialist Chile, 50 Years on
items.content: A socialist/Marxist alliance led by Salvador Allende won power through the democratic electoral process in Chile. This government was overthrown through a military coup led by General Pinochet engineered by the CIA. Massive human rights violations followed in the 17-year military dictatorship. Now Pinochet is thrown into the dustbin of history while Allende remains an icon of the socialist movement.

items.title: Badal shaped Punjab's political trajectory
items.content: The recent death of former CM Parkash Singh Badal marked the end of an era in Akali and Punjab politics. He was the last member of the third generation of Akali politicians. The new generation of Akali politicians must transcend narrow visions of electoral politics.

items.title: Big Questions over India's 'Green Hydrogen' project
items.content: That hydrogen is a problematic green energy resource as an alternative to fossil fuels is not generally recognised. This obfuscation characterises the Indian government's 'green' hydrogen mission too.

items.title: Letter: It's a myth that capitalism is run on free-market rules
items.content: It is a myth that a capitalist economy is governed by the logic of free market competition. The late Joan Robinson of Cambridge University had long ago exploded the myth in her pioneering work 'The Economics of Imperfect Competition'.
